what_it_does: A companion findings doc on how a structural finding (exact, deterministic, auto-actable) and a semantic finding (a fuzzy 4B-swarm candidate, positive-only) live in one coherence finding-model without the fuzzy class corroding the exact one. It specifies the record-shape delta, the positive-only-as-a-leg discipline, structural-semantic disagreement as a derived meta-finding, the two-level confirmation flow, and how the build loop consumes both classes.
observations: Proposes a +2-field schema delta on the AREA-1 record: source: structural|semantic (a discriminator) and a semantic-only derived confidence; evidence is already polymorphic enough to carry an AST reach-result or a swarm JSON.
Expresses positive-only as a fourth propose-only leg in AREA-3's directional hybrid: semantic may ADD a candidate but never demote/close/confirm-live; a 4B 'looks fine' is discarded, never recorded.
Models structural-semantic disagreement as a read-time-derived meta-finding over two source-tagged events at one address, keeping the own/reflect split (nothing new owned).
Grounds disagreement in the measured 3/82 false-wires from AREA-3 (including /api/mockup-feedback) as the first live disagreement findings.

Separates two confirmation levels: jury agreement raises quality but stays candidate; promotion to actionable is a separate act by a stronger authority (Claude Code agent or Tim), recorded as an owned disposition.
Argues the loop discriminates by DISPOSITION not SOURCE: a semantic finding is born action != finish, so AREA-4's existing 'if action != finish: continue' absorbs both classes.

Corrects two sibling-area proposals: AREA-4's detector-re-run closure does not transfer to semantic (a 4B re-read can't close), and AREA-1's content-hash dedup key is wrong for semantic (coarse key on finding-type+target+source instead).
standouts: The disagreement meta-finding as the spine: the one place the fuzzy class bears on the exact class without violating positive-only.
The coarse-key correction forced by LLM non-determinism, so a confirmed disposition survives re-worded re-reads.
The single composition diagram tying record-shape, the four legs, the confirm-path, and the loop together.
conventions: Composes against the sibling AREA-1..6 docs rather than re-inventing, citing each precedent.
Closes with a 'yes, but actually' list of corrections to anchor and sibling areas.
notes: If briefing someone: one record shape with a +1 discriminator; the discriminator decides which lane a finding may travel, never whether it is real; semantic adds only candidates and its single power over the structural skeleton is to raise a disagreement for a stronger judge.
questions: Where exactly does the detector-to-finding ingestion adapter that discards 'looks fine' live in the codebase?
How is the read-time disagreement derivation computed and surfaced, given it is proposed as your-idea?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches its stated area: it is the join — the record shape and disciplines that carry both detector classes in one model — not a detector doc or a substrate doc.
apparent_themes: Positive-only enforced by record shape and lane, not by remembered rule
Disagreement as the highest-value composed finding
intention_signals: It is trying to make the two-class composition safe-by-construction so a cheap fuzzy class cannot corrupt the trustworthy exact class, while keeping the loop unchanged and reusing the existing disposition substrate.
novelty: The propose-only-fourth-leg framing and the coarse-dedup-key correction forced by sampling non-determinism are distinctive design moves.
contribution: Contributes the unified finding-record schema delta, the positive-only-as-a-leg composition, the derived disagreement meta-finding, the two-level confirmation flow, and the disposition-not-source loop-consumption model.
summary_for_embedding: A composition findings doc defining one coherence finding-model carrying both structural and semantic findings via a source discriminator, a propose-only semantic leg, a derived disagreement meta-finding, and a two-level confirm path.
intention_for_embedding: To compose the exact structural and fuzzy semantic detector classes into a single finding-model without the fuzzy class corroding the trustworthy one.
